phases.name: ReferenceRegistry walking skeleton
phases.description: Build the pure-domain ReferenceRegistry that the resolver and detection pipeline will consume in later waves.

steps.name: Stub buildRegistry empty contract
criteria: Importing the registry module no longer fails
buildRegistry on empty config produces empty byName
buildRegistry on empty config produces empty byFilePath
Returned registry version is strictly greater than zero
steps.scenario: Loading state with no aggregated configuration renders no tokens and no crash
steps.scenario_name: buildRegistry returns an empty registry when given an empty aggregated config
steps.implementation_notes: Create the module with the public surface from architecture sec 6.1: types RefType, RegistryEntry, ReferenceRegistry; pure functions buildRegistry, lookupByName, lookupByPath. Empty implementation: buildRegistry returns empty maps and version prevVersion+1.

steps.name: Index AggregatedConfig into byName
criteria: Building from walkingSkeletonConfig and looking up nw-bdd-requirements returns one entry
Returned entry has type skill scope user name nw-bdd-requirements
lookupByName for an unknown name still returns an empty list
buildRegistry remains synchronous and pure
steps.scenario: lookupByName returns the user-scope skill nw-bdd-requirements as a single live entry
steps.scenario_name: lookupByName returns the user-scope skill nw-bdd-requirements as a single live entry
steps.implementation_notes: Walk all seven AggregatedConfig collections (agents parsed branch only commands skills hooks mcpServers rules plugins) and produce RegistryEntry per item keyed in byName as a multimap. Mirror configAggregator.ts style.

steps.name: Populate byFilePath with normalisation
criteria: lookupByPath with the absolute href returns a non-null entry
Returned entry has type skill name nw-bdd-requirements
Path equivalence is structural across forms
lookupByPath returns null for an unknown path
steps.scenario: lookupByPath resolves the absolute markdown link href to the same registry entry
steps.scenario_name: lookupByPath resolves the absolute markdown link href to the same registry entry
steps.implementation_notes: Add a private normalisePath string transform handling backslash forward slash conversion leading dot slash strip interior dot slash collapse trailing slash strip. Apply at insert and lookup. Do not call node:os.

steps.name: Preserve multi-scope name collisions
criteria: Looking up release in ambiguousReleaseConfig returns at least two candidates
Returned candidate scopes include both project and user
Single-entry lookups still return exactly one entry
Candidate ordering is deterministic across builds
steps.scenario: lookupByName for an ambiguous name returns all matching candidates
steps.scenario_name: lookupByName for an ambiguous name returns all matching candidates
steps.implementation_notes: byName multimap from 01-02 should already preserve multiple entries. Behavioural validation step. Do not sort here ordering policy belongs to ScopePrecedence.

steps.name: NFR-2 500-item registry build
criteria: make500ItemConfig returns an AggregatedConfig with 500 items
buildRegistry on the 500-item config produces a registry with byName size 500
Build completes within 100 ms wall-clock
buildRegistry returns a value not a Promise
All previously-live scenarios continue to pass
steps.scenario: buildRegistry with 500 items completes synchronously and produces the correct entry count
steps.scenario_name: buildRegistry with 500 items completes synchronously and produces the correct entry count
steps.implementation_notes: Add make500ItemConfig helper to fixtures.ts producing 100 each of skills commands agents hooks mcpServers with unique names. Un-skip the scenario. Use performance.now for elapsed timing.
